phases.name: Orchestration Layer (LangGraph)
phases.summary: LangGraph StateGraph wired up routing between retrieval, order-lookup, and escalation branches, verified end-to-end with stub tools that echo input.

decisions.decision: Stub tools used to verify control flow before real tool logic (Phase 7) is built
decisions.rationale: So orchestration bugs and tool bugs aren't debugged simultaneously.

phases.name: Tools & Sub-agents
phases.summary: Started with the order-lookup tool. Read project-context/product-spec.md and project-context/addendum-notes-2026-07-08.md for the tool contract. Implemented the parts of src/tools/order_lookup.py independent of API auth details (input validation, response shape, error taxonomy, audit logging). Paused before implementing the HTTP client because product-spec.md and the addendum describe two different, mutually exclusive integration contracts for the Order Management API. Logged as ISSUE-001 instead of guessing. Escalation tool and orchestrator wiring not yet started.

files.description: order-lookup tool -- validation/response-shape/error-taxonomy/audit-logging implemented; HTTP client construction blocked on ISSUE-001

decisions.decision: Implemented the auth-independent parts of the tool now rather than blocking the whole file
decisions.rationale: Input validation, response shape, error types, and audit logging don't depend on which auth contract is correct, so they're safe to build and review now.

decisions.decision: Did not choose between the API-key (product-spec.md) and OAuth2 (addendum) auth contracts despite the addendum appearing newer/more authoritative
decisions.rationale: Per the project's flag-and-stop policy: an autonomous guess that turns out wrong is more expensive to unwind later than a short pause to confirm with the user/platform team now.

remaining_for_phase: Wire order-lookup and escalation tools into src/orchestrator/graph.py, one at a time
